## Roster

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=73 "Edit section: Roster")]

Main article: [List of current UFC fighters](/wiki/List_of_current_UFC_fighters "List of current UFC fighters")

As of October 20, 2020, the UFC roster consisted of fighters from 71 countries.[[410]](#cite_note-412) As of June 14, 2025, there are 596 fighters on the UFC roster, a plurality of which, 207 or 35%, are American. Despite having the largest pool of fighters, there are no male American champions in the UFC as of June 22, 2025.[[411]](#cite_note-413)

### Fighter salaries and contracts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=74 "Edit section: Fighter salaries and contracts")]

UFC fighters are paid a substantially lower share of revenue than athletes in comparable sports,[[412]](#cite_note-414)[[413]](#cite_note-415) and other large MMA promotions.[[414]](#cite_note-416) UFC fighters are paid per fight,[[415]](#cite_note-417) with amounts depending on how well-known the fighters are and how well sponsored a fighter and an event is. Fighters will typically get paid money to fight, called show money, with an additional bonus if they win, called a win bonus. Despite not being officially confirmed by the UFC, [Justin Gaethje](/wiki/Justin_Gaethje "Justin Gaethje") revealed in January 2019 that headlining an event nets an additional $25,000 per fighter.[[416]](#cite_note-418) While fighters new to the UFC can make as little as about $10,000 per fight (without a win bonus), more established fighters have made as much as $500,000 to $1 million per fight. Occasionally, fighters will earn more. For example, at [UFC 202](/wiki/UFC_202 "UFC 202"), [Conor McGregor](/wiki/Conor_McGregor "Conor McGregor") made a reported $3 million, a UFC record for one fight, while his opponent [Nate Diaz](/wiki/Nate_Diaz "Nate Diaz") made a reported $2 million. The combined $5 million made between the fighters was the largest combined fight purse in UFC history.[[417]](#cite_note-419)

Cash bonuses are also awarded for "Fight of the Night" and "Performance of the Night" (formerly awarded separately as "Knockout of the Night").[[418]](#cite_note-420) The size of these bonuses can sometimes be US$80,000 (but are normally US$50,000). For less well-known fighters, they can be several times larger than the contracted amount for the fight.[[419]](#cite_note-421) Contracted amounts generally have to be declared to the state athletic commission; however, the UFC also pays undisclosed locker-room bonuses to fighters.[[420]](#cite_note-422) In early 2021, Dana White revealed that these bonuses vary from $4,000 to $25,000.[[421]](#cite_note-423) In recent years, UFC fighters' contracts and merchandising rights have been the subject of dispute between fighters (represented by growing the Mixed Martial Arts Fighters Association) and UFC, which has attempted to defend existing regulations.[[422]](#cite_note-424)

UFC is contractually bound to offer every fighter three fights per calendar year and if they don't, the organization has to pay the fighter for the lacking fights. For example, if the UFC offers a fighter only one fight during the year, they have to pay the fighter for two additional fights. However, if a fight is offered but turned down by the fighter, it is still counted as an offered fight from the contractual viewpoint.[[423]](#cite_note-425)

Starting at [UFC 273](/wiki/UFC_273 "UFC 273"), three "Fan Bonus Of The Night", for every pay-per-view event, were awarded by [Crypto.com](/wiki/Crypto.com "Crypto.com"), as part of Crypto.com UFC sponsorship incentive fight kits deal,[[424]](#cite_note-426) for every pay-per-view event starting UFC 273. Viewers vote up to three times per pay-per-view on Crypto.com/FanBonus, starting from the opening of the PPV preliminary card and ending an hour after the conclusion of the main card. The bonuses are paid in [bitcoin](/wiki/Bitcoin "Bitcoin") in US dollars ranging from US$30,000 for first place, US$20,000 for second place, and US$10,000 for third place.[[425]](#cite_note-427) From 2023, the times these awards were given less frequent and not for every pay-per-view for unknown reasons.

Fighters are allowed to compete outside of the promotion in non-striking sports such as [Brazilian jiu-jitsu](/wiki/Brazilian_jiu-jitsu "Brazilian jiu-jitsu") and [wrestling](/wiki/Amateur_wrestling "Amateur wrestling"), provided they do not have a scheduled UFC bout within eight weeks of the competition.[[426]](#cite_note-j370-428)

## UFC events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=78 "Edit section: UFC events")]

Main article: [List of UFC events](/wiki/List_of_UFC_events "List of UFC events")

MMA journalists and fans have criticized the UFC for putting on too many shows and thus diluting the quality of their product.[[447]](#cite_note-449)

### Production team

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=79 "Edit section: Production team")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/38/AriannyLopez.png/250px-AriannyLopez.png)](/wiki/File:AriannyLopez.png)

Octagon girl [Arianny Celeste](/wiki/Arianny_Celeste "Arianny Celeste")

Comedian, Brazilian jiu-jitsu and Taekwondo black belt[[448]](#cite_note-450) [Joe Rogan](/wiki/Joe_Rogan "Joe Rogan") teams up with [play-by-play](/wiki/Sports_commentator "Sports commentator") announcer [Jon Anik](/wiki/Jon_Anik "Jon Anik"), [UFC Hall of Famer](/wiki/UFC_Hall_of_Fame "UFC Hall of Fame") [Daniel Cormier](/wiki/Daniel_Cormier "Daniel Cormier") and [Megan Olivi](/wiki/Megan_Olivi "Megan Olivi") to provide commentary during broadcasts of most UFC events in the US. For 20 years[[c]](#cite_note-451) Rogan and [Mike Goldberg](/wiki/Mike_Goldberg "Mike Goldberg") provided commentary at live events.[[449]](#cite_note-452) The "Veteran Voice of the Octagon" is announcer [Bruce Buffer](/wiki/Bruce_Buffer "Bruce Buffer").[[450]](#cite_note-453) [Arianny Celeste](/wiki/Arianny_Celeste "Arianny Celeste"), Rachelle Leah, [Brittney Palmer](/wiki/Brittney_Palmer "Brittney Palmer"), Carly Baker, Vanessa Hanson, Chrissy Blair, Jhenny Andrade, Camila Oliveira, Luciana Andrade, Jamilette Gaxiola, and Red Dela Cruz are [Octagon girls](/wiki/Ring_girl "Ring girl").[[451]](#cite_note-454) Each fighter is assigned a [cutman](/wiki/Cutman "Cutman") by the promotion who cares for the fighter before the fight and in between rounds. [Jacob "Stitch" Duran](/wiki/Jacob_Duran "Jacob Duran") was one of the best known cutmen working for the organization.[[452]](#cite_note-455) Matches are made by matchmakers, and VP of Talent Relations, Joe Silva and Sean Shelby.[[453]](#cite_note-456)

## Media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=82 "Edit section: Media")]

### Television

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=83 "Edit section: Television")]

#### Television programs produced by UFC

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=84 "Edit section: Television programs produced by UFC")]

* *UFC Connected* is a magazine style television show. In 2018 the UFC announced a monthly show to be hosted by UK presenter [Layla Anna-Lee](/wiki/Layla_Anna-Lee "Layla Anna-Lee")[[504]](#cite_note-507) With regular contributions from Dan Hardy and John Gooden, UFC Connected features a behind the scenes look at the UFC and its athletes. The show is also shown on [BT Sport](/wiki/BT_Sport "BT Sport") in the UK.[[505]](#cite_note-508)
* *UFC Now* is a long running[[506]](#cite_note-509) weekly [television show](/wiki/Television_show "Television show") presented by [Karyn Bryant](/wiki/Karyn_Bryant "Karyn Bryant").[[507]](#cite_note-510)[[508]](#cite_note-511) The show has grown in popularity over the years where UFC fighters such as [Cub Swanson](/wiki/Cub_Swanson "Cub Swanson"),[[509]](#cite_note-512) [Kenny Florian](/wiki/Kenny_Florian "Kenny Florian")[[510]](#cite_note-513) and [Alan Jouban](/wiki/Alan_Jouban "Alan Jouban")[[511]](#cite_note-514) appear as regular guests and analysts. The show is available on [UFC Fight Pass](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Pass "UFC Fight Pass") and is aired on [BT Sport](/wiki/BT_Sport "BT Sport")[[512]](#cite_note-515) in the UK. The show has regular segments such as breaking down the latest fighters for up and coming events, a roundhouse quiz, a top 5 selection and a rapid fire taking questions from fans via various social media platforms. Other guests who have regularly appeared are [Michael Bisping](/wiki/Michael_Bisping "Michael Bisping"),[[513]](#cite_note-516) [Tatiana Suarez](/wiki/Tatiana_Suarez "Tatiana Suarez"),[[514]](#cite_note-517) [Daniel Cormier](/wiki/Daniel_Cormier "Daniel Cormier"),[[515]](#cite_note-518) [Brendan Schaub](/wiki/Brendan_Schaub "Brendan Schaub"),[[516]](#cite_note-519) [Brian Ortega](/wiki/Brian_Ortega "Brian Ortega")[[517]](#cite_note-520) and more throughout the years. It was reported in 2016 Schaub will no longer appear due to being banned from the show.[[518]](#cite_note-521)[[519]](#cite_note-522)[[520]](#cite_note-523)[[521]](#cite_note-524)[[522]](#cite_note-525)
* *UFC Tonight* is a television series produced by [Fox Sports 1](/wiki/Fox_Sports_1 "Fox Sports 1") and the UFC. Hosted by [Kenny Florian](/wiki/Kenny_Florian "Kenny Florian") and Karyn Bryant, the program features the latest news, highlights, and analysis from the UFC. This program was carried over to FS1 from Fuel TV (now [Fox Sports 2](/wiki/Fox_Sports_2 "Fox Sports 2")).[[523]](#cite_note-MMAweekleyUFC-526)
* *UFC Unleashed* is a television series produced by [Spike TV](/wiki/Spike_TV "Spike TV") and the UFC. It features matches from past UFC events. Episodes are one hour in length, showing several UFC bouts and "[best of](/wiki/Best_of "Best of")" compilations of popular fighters such as [Chuck Liddell](/wiki/Chuck_Liddell "Chuck Liddell") and [Randy Couture](/wiki/Randy_Couture "Randy Couture"). In Australia, the show can be seen on Weeknights on [FX](/wiki/FX_Australia "FX Australia"). In Sweden, the show can be seen on [TV4 Sport](/wiki/TV4_Sport "TV4 Sport"). In Portugal, the show can be seen on [Sic Radical](/wiki/Sic_Radical "Sic Radical"). In Germany, the show can be seen on DSF. In the United Kingdom, the show can be seen on [ESPN UK](/wiki/ESPN_UK "ESPN UK") every Thursday at 10 pm. In Denmark, the show can be seen on [Canal 8 Sport](/wiki/Canal_8_Sport "Canal 8 Sport") and [Canal 9](/wiki/Canal_9_(Denmark) "Canal 9 (Denmark)"). In Finland, the show can be seen on [MTV3 MAX](/wiki/MTV3_MAX "MTV3 MAX"). In France, the show can be seen on [RTL9](/wiki/RTL9 "RTL9"). In Brazil the show can be seen on [Combate](/wiki/Combate "Combate") In Middle East, the show can be seen on [ShowSports](/wiki/ShowSports "ShowSports") and [FX Middle East](/wiki/FX_(TV_channel)#Middle_East "FX (TV channel)").[[524]](#cite_note-527)
* *[Fox UFC](/wiki/Fox_UFC "Fox UFC")* (until 2018)
* *[UFC All Access](/wiki/UFC_All_Access "UFC All Access")*
* *[UFC Primetime](/wiki/UFC_Primetime "UFC Primetime")*
* *[UFC Ultimate Insider](/wiki/UFC_Ultimate_Insider "UFC Ultimate Insider")*
* *[The Ultimate Fighter](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter "The Ultimate Fighter")*
* *UFC on ESPN/ABC* (2019–2025)
* *UFC on CBS/Paramount+* (2026–present)

#### Appearances on other television programs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=85 "Edit section: Appearances on other television programs")]

* *[Friends](/wiki/Friends "Friends")* Season 3, Episode 24 "The One with the Ultimate Fighting Champion" is based around the UFC and features [Jon Favreau](/wiki/Jon_Favreau "Jon Favreau") as Pete Becker, [Monica](/wiki/Monica_Geller "Monica Geller")'s millionaire boyfriend who seeks to become the "Ultimate Fighting Champion", losing his first fight due to his opponent "standing on [his] neck", and the second to a man who "Trains by traveling to Iran and pulling the arms off of thieves". In his third fight, Pete loses to a fighter who "goes for his favorite area", causing [Ross](/wiki/Ross_Geller "Ross Geller") to note Pete can no longer have kids.[[525]](#cite_note-528)
* *[The Simpsons](/wiki/The_Simpsons "The Simpsons")* Season 21, Episode 2 "[The Great Wife Hope](/wiki/The_Great_Wife_Hope "The Great Wife Hope")" is largely based around the UFC[[526]](#cite_note-529) where [Marge](/wiki/Marge_Simpson "Marge Simpson") ends up fighting in the octagon at the end of the episode. UFC president White said "We like fighting, but to make it sound like the UFC crowd is this bloodthirsty crowd that shows up and just want to see people get their (butts) kicked – that's not true. That's the stigma that the mainstream has of us".[[527]](#cite_note-530) [Chuck Liddell](/wiki/Chuck_Liddell "Chuck Liddell") starred in the episode.[[528]](#cite_note-531) A later reference was featured in the episode "[The Way of the Dog](/wiki/The_Way_of_the_Dog "The Way of the Dog")" from Season 31, Episode 22 which aired on May 17, 2020, where [Homer](/wiki/Homer_Simpson "Homer Simpson") wanted to stay in to watch [UFC 243](/wiki/UFC_243 "UFC 243") and said "Not tomorrow! Tomorrow is UFC fight 243",[[529]](#cite_note-532) he was later seen watching the event on his phone at the seminar. UFC president White also shared the clip on his social media.[[530]](#cite_note-533)
* *[Beavis and Butt-Head](/wiki/Beavis_and_Butt-Head "Beavis and Butt-Head")* Season 8, Episode 14. It was first announced in 2011 that *Beavis and Butt-Head* would feature UFC in their comeback season.[[531]](#cite_note-534)[[532]](#cite_note-535)[[533]](#cite_note-536) In the episode "Holding", you see [Beavis](/wiki/Beavis "Beavis") and [Butt-Head](/wiki/Butt-Head "Butt-Head") watching the UFC of which creator [Mike Judge](/wiki/Mike_Judge "Mike Judge") first announced saying "They're also watching UFC fights" at a comic-con that year.[[534]](#cite_note-537) The episode first aired on` December 8, 2011, on [MTV](/wiki/MTV "MTV"). UFC president White is a known fan of the show and has referenced *Beavis and Butt-Head* in the past[[535]](#cite_note-538) as well as hanging out with creator Mike Judge in 2010 where it was reported "White says the UFC and Judge will be working together".[[536]](#cite_note-539) In 2016, Judge created an official [UFC 200](/wiki/UFC_200 "UFC 200") animated short featuring [Daniel Cormier](/wiki/Daniel_Cormier "Daniel Cormier") and [Jon Jones](/wiki/Jon_Jones "Jon Jones").[[537]](#cite_note-540)

In January 2007, Zuffa and video game developer/publisher [THQ](/wiki/THQ "THQ") announced a license agreement giving THQ worldwide rights to develop titles under the UFC brand. The agreement gives THQ exclusive rights to current and next-generation consoles as well as to PC and handheld titles. Also included are "certain wireless rights" which were not detailed. The licensing agreement was set to expire in 2011, although it appeared to have been extended to 2017. On June 4, 2012, THQ announced they will be giving the license of UFC Undisputed to EA.[[539]](#cite_note-542)

### Action figures

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=88 "Edit section: Action figures")]

#### Round 5

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=89 "Edit section: Round 5")]

The first UFC action figure collectibles were released by Round 5 Corporation in May 2008.[[540]](#cite_note-543) Series one of their figures includes [Quinton "Rampage" Jackson](/wiki/Quinton_%22Rampage%22_Jackson "Quinton \"Rampage\" Jackson"), [Matt Hughes](/wiki/Matt_Hughes_(fighter) "Matt Hughes (fighter)"), [Tito Ortiz](/wiki/Tito_Ortiz "Tito Ortiz"), and [Randy Couture](/wiki/Randy_Couture "Randy Couture"). Series two (released on November 10, 2008) includes [Wanderlei Silva](/wiki/Wanderlei_Silva "Wanderlei Silva"), [Sean Sherk](/wiki/Sean_Sherk "Sean Sherk"), [Rich Franklin](/wiki/Rich_Franklin "Rich Franklin"), and [Anderson Silva](/wiki/Anderson_Silva "Anderson Silva").[[541]](#cite_note-544)

In July 2009, Round 5 acquired the UFC license through Jakks Pacific and subsequently released five more series under the UFC and Pride brands. Two packs were released in August 2010 and include a UFC Octagon cage and Pride ring display stand. Limited edition versions include fabric walk-out tees or paint variations and are limited in number with foil and holographic packaging variances. Special edition and exclusive versions have been released at various UFC Fan Expo events.[[542]](#cite_note-545)

#### Jakks Pacific

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=90 "Edit section: Jakks Pacific")]

On June 10, 2008, it was announced that UFC had signed an exclusive four-year contract with [Jakks Pacific](/wiki/Jakks_Pacific "Jakks Pacific") to create action figures for UFC. As of 2009[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit) the schedule envisages the release of these figures in November 2009. They have currently been 8 series released and they feature special Legends, Pride, and WEC style figures as well. Three 2 packs series have also been released, as well as several expo and internet exclusives. There are also several different octagon cage playsets that have been released, including the "Octagon Playset", "Official Scale Octagon Playset", and "Electronic Reaction Octagon Playset". A Pridestyle ring playset was also originally planned; however, no news have been given on its status or release date since then.[[543]](#cite_note-546)[[544]](#cite_note-547)[[545]](#cite_note-548)

#### Jazwares

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=91 "Edit section: Jazwares")]

In 2020, the UFC and toy company [Jazwares](/wiki/Jazwares "Jazwares") entered into an agreement to release a new line of UFC action figures.[[550]](#cite_note-553) The first wave of Jazwares' UFC figures were scheduled to be released in Fall 2020.

### DVDs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=92 "Edit section: DVDs")]

Various UFC events have been released onto DVD. [UFC 23](/wiki/UFC_23 "UFC 23") through [UFC 29](/wiki/UFC_29 "UFC 29") were not released in the US on home video or DVD by SEG. They have since been released onto boxsets which feature around 10 events each set, in chronological order.[[551]](#cite_note-554)

### PlayStation Network and Xbox Live

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=93 "Edit section: PlayStation Network and Xbox Live")]

UFC on-demand content launched for [Xbox 360](/wiki/Xbox_360 "Xbox 360") on December 20, 2011. Subscribers were able to view pay-per-view events in high definition, connect with friends to predict fight results, and have the ability to compare fighter statistics and records.[[552]](#cite_note-555) The [UFC Fight Pass](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Pass "UFC Fight Pass") application was also planned for [PlayStation 4](/wiki/PlayStation_4 "PlayStation 4") in early 2015.[[553]](#cite_note-556) In 2015, UFC Fight Pass became available on the Xbox One.[[554]](#cite_note-557) In December 2022, a PlayStation version of the app was released.

### NFTs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=94 "Edit section: NFTs")]

In August 2021, the UFC began to release NFTs to commemorate big fights, milestones and moments via their official trading card partner, [Panini America](/wiki/Panini_America "Panini America").[[555]](#cite_note-558)

## International broadcasters

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=95 "Edit section: International broadcasters")]

The UFC's PPV events are broadcast live on [pay-per-view](/wiki/ESPN%2B "ESPN+") in the US and [TNT Sports Box Office](/wiki/TNT_Sports_Box_Office "TNT Sports Box Office") in the United Kingdom.

### United Kingdom

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=96 "Edit section: United Kingdom")]

In the UK; BT Sport's first ever live event was August 3, 2013 [UFC 163](/wiki/UFC_163 "UFC 163") headlined by [José Aldo](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Aldo "José Aldo") and [The Korean Zombie](/wiki/The_Korean_Zombie "The Korean Zombie").[[556]](#cite_note-559) BT Sport were the first major broadcaster in the UK for the UFC where previously they had small coverage on [Bravo](/wiki/Bravo_(British_TV_channel) "Bravo (British TV channel)"), [Setanta Sports](/wiki/Setanta_Sports "Setanta Sports")[[557]](#cite_note-560) and finally [ESPN UK](/wiki/ESPN_in_the_United_Kingdom "ESPN in the United Kingdom") in August, 2012[[558]](#cite_note-561) until the BT Sport deal was in place.

As of August 2025, TNT Sports, the successor of BT Sport, holds exclusive rights to UFC in the United Kingdom.

### Mexico

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=97 "Edit section: Mexico")]

Events are broadcast live on [Fighting Sports Network](/wiki/Televisa "Televisa") in Pay TV (Cable and Satellite) of SKY Satellite. Free TV Channels in Mexico, [Fox Sports](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Latin_American_TV_network) "Fox Sports (Latin American TV network)") and [Fox Premium](/wiki/Fox_Premium "Fox Premium") in Pay TV (Cable and Satellite) Central America are broadcast with tape-delay.

### Brazil

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=98 "Edit section: Brazil")]

In Brazil, events are broadcast live on [open television](/wiki/Free-to-air "Free-to-air") through [Rede Bandeirantes](/wiki/Rede_Bandeirantes "Rede Bandeirantes"), which transmits the Fight Night events and preliminary cards of main events, while the numbered events are on [Fight Pass](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Pass "UFC Fight Pass").[[559]](#cite_note-www.uol.com.br-562) Previously, [Rede Globo](/wiki/Rede_Globo "Rede Globo") broadcast it on its own martial arts channel [Combate](https://pt.wikipedia.org/wiki/Combate_(canal_de_televis%C3%A3o) "pt:Combate (canal de televisão)") from 2011 to 2022, with select events on its broadcast network.[[559]](#cite_note-www.uol.com.br-562)

### Asia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=99 "Edit section: Asia")]

In Southeast Asia (exclude the Philippines), Hong Kong, and Papua New Guinea, UFC events are broadcast on [Fox Movies Premium](/wiki/Fox_Movies_Premium "Fox Movies Premium") (2012–2013)[[560]](#cite_note-563) and [Fox Sports](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Asia "Fox Sports Asia")[[561]](#cite_note-564) (2013–2021).[[562]](#cite_note-565)

In [India](/wiki/India "India"), events are broadcast on [Sony Ten 2](/wiki/Sony_Ten "Sony Ten"), Sony Ten 1, Sony Ten 1 HD and Sony Ten 2 HD.[[563]](#cite_note-566)

In the [Philippines](/wiki/Philippines "Philippines"), UFC was also aired on [Balls](/wiki/Balls_(TV_channel) "Balls (TV channel)") (now [ABS-CBN Sports + Action HD](/wiki/ABS-CBN_Sports_and_Action "ABS-CBN Sports and Action")) from 2009 until 2015, since moved to [Sports5](/wiki/Sports5 "Sports5") (including [TV5](/wiki/The_5_Network "The 5 Network"), [AksyonTV](/wiki/AksyonTV "AksyonTV") and [Hyper](/wiki/Hyper_(TV_channel) "Hyper (TV channel)") on Channel 91 (SD) and 261 (HD) via [Cignal](/wiki/Cignal "Cignal")) starting January 3, 2016 until December 31, 2018, and is currently airing on [TAP Digital Media Ventures Corporation](/wiki/TAP_Digital_Media_Ventures_Corporation "TAP Digital Media Ventures Corporation")-owned sports cable television channel [Premier Sports](/wiki/Premier_Sports_(Philippine_TV_channel) "Premier Sports (Philippine TV channel)") since October 2021.[[564]](#cite_note-567)[[565]](#cite_note-568)[[566]](#cite_note-569)[[567]](#cite_note-570)

In [Sri Lanka](/wiki/Sri_Lanka "Sri Lanka"), UFC events have been broadcast on several television networks over the years. Previously, UFC events were aired on various channels from 2012 to 2017, including [Dialog TV](/wiki/Dialog_TV "Dialog TV") and [PEO TV](/wiki/Peo_TV "Peo TV").[[568]](#cite_note-571)[[569]](#cite_note-572) Starting January 15, 2018, [Sony Sports Ten 2](/w/index.php?title=Sony_Sports_Ten_2&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sony Sports Ten 2 (page does not exist)") became the primary broadcaster for UFC events in Sri Lanka, airing the events in English.[[570]](#cite_note-573) Additionally, UFC events have been available via [UFC Fight Pass](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Pass "UFC Fight Pass"), which offers live streaming of events and on-demand content for viewers in Sri Lanka.[[571]](#cite_note-574)

In [Indonesia](/wiki/Indonesia "Indonesia"), UFC events are currently broadcast on [tvOne](/wiki/TvOne_(Indonesia) "TvOne (Indonesia)") starting January 15, 2018;[[572]](#cite_note-575) previously, this events was also broadcast on [RCTI](/wiki/RCTI "RCTI"),[[573]](#cite_note-576) [iNews](/wiki/INews "INews")[[574]](#cite_note-577) since 2012 until the end of 2017.[[575]](#cite_note-578)

### United Arab Emirates

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=100 "Edit section: United Arab Emirates")]

On April 13, 2019, UFC's event was broadcast on [Abu Dhabi Media](/wiki/Abu_Dhabi_Media "Abu Dhabi Media").[[576]](#cite_note-579)

### Poland

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=101 "Edit section: Poland")]

In Poland since 2018 the official broadcaster of UFC it's [Polsat Sport](/wiki/Polsat_Sport "Polsat Sport").[[577]](#cite_note-580) Previously the UFC events in this nation are broadcast by [Orange Sport](/wiki/Orange_Sport_(Poland) "Orange Sport (Poland)") and [Extreme Sports Channel](/wiki/Extreme_Sports_Channel "Extreme Sports Channel"). The main Polish broadcasters of UFC telecasts, are Andrzej Janisz and Łukasz "Juras" Jurkowski.

### Canada

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=102 "Edit section: Canada")]

In 2024, [Rogers](/wiki/Rogers_Sports_%26_Media "Rogers Sports & Media") successfully bid for the rights to broadcast UFC in Canada on its [Sportsnet](/wiki/Sportsnet "Sportsnet") channels. The UFC was previously on Sportsnet and French broadcaster [TVA](/wiki/TVA_Sports "TVA Sports") from September 2011 through the end of 2014 in Canada.[[578]](#cite_note-581)

### Russia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ultimate_Fighting_Championship&action=edit&section=103 "Edit section: Russia")]

In Russian, UFC events are broadcast on Match TV, a Russian sports channel owned by Gazprom Media, starting 2022.[[579]](#cite_note-582)

Retrieved March 10, 2024. "Then there were the rules changes, including no more head-butting, which negatively affected Coleman's talents. Wrestlers once dominated the sport, because they would put opponents on the ground and pound them with hands and elbows. But as limitations on permissible striking areas increased in the late 1990s, the effectiveness of Coleman's "ground and pound" techniques decreased.  
      
    Suddenly, it wasn't as easy to "take a guy to the ground and pound the (bleep) out of him until he quits," he said.  
      
    "When they took out the head butt, it made the jujitsu game (a form of ground fighting) more effective. It completely changed the sport so for a while there, I was a little bit lost. As fast as I climbed to the top, I fell to the bottom just as fast.""
